prompt: Create a Meridian Offline Commerce & Compliance Intelligence platform that unifies local community delivery operations with job-and-content intelligence for a multi-site retailer running in disconnected environments. Store Managers and Dispatchers use a React web console to maintain store and dark-store profiles, define delivery coverage by community grid and administrative area mapping using locally uploaded boundary files, and configure per-zone rules such as product availability, a $25.00 minimum order threshold, $3.99 standard delivery fee, and delivery windows like 9:00 AM–12:00 PM and 4:00 PM–8:00 PM. Operations Analysts and Recruiters use the same interface to run full-text search across internally stored job posts, operational notices, and vendor bulletins by title, body, author, and tags, with highlighted matches and filters for date range, store, region, and content type; sorting supports newest first, most viewed, and highest reply count. The UI provides clear feedback for offline status, queueing changes locally when the backend is busy, and showing traceable version timelines on any job post or policy note so users can compare what changed since a prior update and roll back within 30 days if needed. Administrators manage role-based access for Store Manager, Dispatcher, Analyst, Recruiter, and Compliance Officer, with exports available only to authorized roles and stamped with a visible watermark including username and export timestamp (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time). The backend uses Symfony to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React client over the local network, enforcing validation, permissions, and deterministic behavior with no external dependencies. MySQL persists operational entities (stores, zones, catalogs, orders metadata) and the analytics warehouse layer with master data and star-schema tables, including product, customer, channel, region, and time dimensions plus sales fact tables; master data CRUD follows coding rules (e.g., region codes are 2–5 uppercase characters), uniqueness checks, referential integrity constraints, and region hierarchy maintenance with effective dates. Search indexing runs as local jobs with incremental updates every 5 minutes, deleting orphaned index entries after 14 days and compacting weekly; index cleanup must never block read queries. For crawling/imported job content, deduplication computes multi-field fingerprints (normalized title, company, location, and first 200 characters) and applies similarity thresholds to auto-merge near-duplicates, while preserving a complete version history of changed fields and timestamps. Anti-bot and stability controls apply to any internal scraping against permitted intranet sources: local proxy pools with auto-switching, rotating user-agent/header templates, per-source rate limits (max 30 requests/minute) with randomized waits, and detection of CAPTCHA/redirect/ban patterns that triggers self-healing actions (degrade to metadata-only, switch source, or pause for 60 minutes). Data governance is enforced with sensitive-data classification, masking of fields like SSNs to last 4 digits, field-level access control, and encrypted storage for classified fields using locally managed keys rotated every 90 days; retention policies support candidate data deletion or irreversible anonymization after 365 days, consent records are stored with immutable timestamps, and compliance audit reports can be generated offline showing access, export, and deletion events with tamper-evident hashing.
